narration: We sort by start time, and if starts tie, we put the interval with the bigger end first.
callout: The bigger covering interval should come first when starts tie or overlap closely.

narration: We track the farthest end seen so far. When [2, 8] appears, the farthest end becomes 8.
decision: A new farther end means this interval is not covered.

narration: When [3, 6] ends at 6, it does not go past 8, so it is covered and should not be counted.
callout: If an interval does not extend farther, a stronger interval already covers it.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Why does sorting tied starts with larger ends first matter here?
